title: May Half-Term Flights 2026: Book Before Prices Jump
subtitle: May 23–31 half-term fares are still reasonable — but not for much longer
airport_names: Gatwick, Stansted, Manchester, Birmingham, Bristol, Edinburgh
meta: Find cheap flights for May half-term 2026 from UK airports. Best destinations, current prices, and how to book before fares spike in April.

sections.heading: Why May half-term is the trickiest school-holiday window
sections.body:
May half-term falls roughly May 23–31 for most English and Welsh schools in 2026, overlapping directly with the Spring Bank Holiday on Monday May 25. That combination — school break plus public holiday — makes it the most demand-compressed non-summer period of the year.

Unlike Easter, where last-minute prices can sometimes stay manageable on less popular routes, May half-term has a narrower window and a more uniform demand spike. Families with school-age children all want the same dates, and there's no flexibility around the school calendar the way there is for adults-only travel.

The window to book at reasonable prices is closing now. From mid-April, prices will begin repricing sharply. By early May — as the half-term week becomes imminent — anything not already booked will be at premium pricing or sold out.

sections.heading: Best destinations for May half-term 2026
sections.body:
Menorca (MAH) from Gatwick or Manchester — easyJet and Jet2 both serve Menorca in May. Unlike Mallorca and Ibiza, Menorca doesn't reprice as aggressively because it's less famous. Fares currently showing around £65–90 one-way from Gatwick for May 23 departures. The island is quiet, warm (22–25°C), and family-friendly. Book now.

Faro (FAO), Algarve from Bristol, Gatwick or Manchester — Ryanair from Stansted and easyJet from Gatwick are both around £60–85 one-way for May 23. The Algarve in late May is close to ideal: school groups have gone home, it's warm and sunny without August heat, and accommodation is 30–40% cheaper than July. Best overall value for a family break.

Malaga (AGP) from Gatwick or Manchester — easyJet from Gatwick and Jet2 from Manchester are showing £75–105 one-way for May 22–23 departures. The Costa del Sol is fully open in late May, and the drive east to Nerja or west to Estepona is easy. Jet2 from Manchester includes luggage, which matters for a family of four.

Marrakech (RAK) from Gatwick or Manchester — easyJet from Gatwick, currently around £60–85 one-way for May 23 departures. Marrakech in late May (26–32°C) is different and rewarding for older children — the souks, Jardin Majorelle, day trips to the Atlas Mountains. A 3-hour flight that delivers a very different kind of break.

sections.heading: Routes that are already repriced for half-term
sections.body:
Some destinations have already priced in the half-term premium. Palma, Mallorca (PMI) from Gatwick or Manchester is the clearest example: Jet2 from Manchester is already showing £120–150 one-way for May 23, which is the same pricing it holds in peak July. The route is simply too popular to find value this close to half-term.

Alicante (ALC) from Manchester on easyJet is similarly gone — £105–135 one-way for May 23. Not outrageously expensive, but not the deal it should be for early booking. Barcelona (BCN) is even worse: £130–175 one-way from Gatwick for the bank holiday weekend.

If you haven't booked these routes already, the better strategy is to look at their less-famous alternatives. Menorca instead of Mallorca. Faro instead of Alicante. Marrakech instead of Barcelona. The experience quality is comparable or better; the prices are consistently lower because demand doesn't concentrate the same way.

sections.heading: Practical tips: booking May half-term right
sections.body:
Compare Jet2 total prices carefully. Jet2 includes 22kg checked luggage per person, a 10kg carry-on, and seat selection for families. For a family of four, that's potentially £120–160 in bag fees saved versus Ryanair or easyJet. On routes Jet2 serves — primarily from Manchester, Leeds, Birmingham — they're often the best total value.

Saturday May 23 vs Friday May 22. Prices on Friday May 22 (the last school day for many) are lower than Saturday May 23. If your children finish school at noon on Friday and you can get to the airport, the Friday departure is meaningfully cheaper on most routes — £25–50 per person less.

Check accommodation before locking in flights. May half-term accommodation in the Algarve, Menorca and Mallorca is in high demand. The worst outcome is finding cheap flights and then discovering that all suitable accommodation is either gone or wildly priced. Confirm a hotel or villa first, then book flights around it.

Return on Sunday May 31 or Monday June 1, not Saturday May 30. The Saturday return is peak pricing — everyone flying Saturday out wants to return the following Saturday. Sunday or Monday returns are consistently £30–60 per person cheaper on popular sun routes, with similarly lower airport crowding.
